summary: Ukrainian forces continue to repel massive Shahed drone waves across the western, central and northern regions, reporting over 700 drones downed and several casualties. Russian operations include coordinated drone swarms, missile launches and KAB rockets on multiple fronts, with intensified pressure on Kyiv and Kharkiv. Civilian support drives fundraising efforts, while international attention remains focused on the escalating air campaign.
key_points: Massive Shahed drone attacks across western and central Ukraine, >700 downed.
Russian use of strategic aviation, ballistic missiles and KAB rockets in coordinated strikes.
Casualties reported: at least 6 civilians killed and dozens injured.
Ukrainian air defenses and REB units actively intercepting drones on all regions.
No territorial changes reported; front lines remain static.
casualties_mentioned: At least 6 civilian deaths and multiple injuries reported in recent attacks.

sections.executive_summary: Ukrainian air defenses are under intense pressure from coordinated Russian drone and missile barrages, especially targeting the west and Kyiv region. Over 700 hostile UAVs have been shot down, but attacks continue with the threat of a second wave involving strategic aircraft. Casualties remain modest relative to the scale of attacks, and front lines are unchanged.

ukraine.title: Ukraine
ukraine.subtitle: Ukrainian defensive operations and strikes
points: Air defense units across all oblasts reported shooting down 710 Shahed, Gerbera and Ittilmas drones on 13 May.
Ukrainian REB, mobile fire groups and anti‑air artillery engaged and suppressed multiple drone swarms, limiting damage.
Civilian fundraising campaigns continue to support frontline logistics and morale.

russia.title: Russia
russia.subtitle: Russian military operations and posture
points: Russia launched massed drone attacks from the Black Sea, Belarus and Moldova, followed by a planned second wave with strategic aviation and ballistic missiles.
KAB rocket launches were reported in Sumy, Donetsk, Kharkiv, Dnipropetrovsk and Zaporizhzhia regions.

eastern_front.title: Eastern Front
eastern_front.subtitle: Donetsk & Luhansk combat operations
points: KAB rockets fired into Donetsk region; no specific ground engagements detailed.
Ukrainian air defenses reported interceptions over the eastern oblasts, limiting drone impact.

northern_front.title: Northern Front
northern_front.subtitle: Kharkiv region and Sumy border activity
points: Shahed drones launched from Kherson towards Kharkiv; KAB rockets also reported in Kharkiv and Sumy.
Ukrainian forces continue to defend against aerial incursions while ground positions remain unchanged.

southern_front.title: Southern Front
southern_front.subtitle: Zaporizhzhia & Kherson operations
points: Multiple Shahed attacks reported in Zaporizhzhia and Kherson, causing civilian injuries.
KAB rocket launches in Zaporizhzhia and Kherson added to the pressure on southern defenses.

air_war.title: Air War
air_war.subtitle: Drone, missile and aviation operations
points: Over 892 hostile UAVs of various types (Shahed, Gerbera, Ittilmas, Parody imitators) engaged in a single day.
Strategic aviation and ballistic missiles are expected in a second wave targeting Kyiv and surrounding oblasts.
Ukrainian air force, SAM units and electronic warfare systems reported high interception rates across all regions.

key_developments: Massive Shahed drone wave with >700 downed
Planned second wave with strategic aircraft and missiles
KAB rocket launches on multiple fronts
No territorial shifts despite intense air activity
sections.threat_assessment: The threat level remains high as Russia prepares a second, more lethal wave of attacks involving strategic aviation and ballistic missiles, especially against Kyiv. Ukrainian air defenses are strained but continue to achieve high interception rates.
sections.regional_response: International observers note the escalation, with NATO allies monitoring the increased drone usage and missile launches. No new diplomatic initiatives were reported in the messages.
sections.intelligence_notes: Frequent mentions of cross‑border launch routes via Belarus and Moldova indicate continued exploitation of neighboring airspace. The coordinated timing of drone swarms and KAB rockets suggests a deliberate effort to overwhelm Ukrainian air defenses.
